title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Query Trace: Conditional Aggregation
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql conditional aggregation increment in an audited service-metrics query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Produce multiple defensible measures with CASE or FILTER while preserving one clear denominator.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 5 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. A defensible solution applies Conditional Aggregation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an audited service-metrics query pack.

content.code:
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events
  GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS tickets,
       COALESCE(SUM(effort.minutes), 0) AS minutes
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Conditional Aggregation evidence · Theory Conditional Aggregation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-conditional-aggregation-823piy" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; aggregate grain, denominator, or reconciliation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Conditional Aggregation evidence · Theory Conditional Aggregation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Conditional Aggregation into observable performance evidence. For Build Conditional Aggregation evidence · Theory Conditional Aggregation, keep Conditional Aggregation connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-conditional-aggregation-823piy
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
